refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve an operational availability equal to or greater than the customer-defined target over the agreed evaluation period.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR was mostly atomic but contained some ambiguity around 'meet or exceed' and 'operational availability target'. Refining it clarifies the requirement as a measurable performance metric without implying any solution or implementation. It remains solution-neutral and focused on what the system must achieve.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the operational availability of the NGHE solution over the agreed evaluation period and compare it against the customer-defined target to verify compliance.

candidate_dps: Implement redundancy and failover mechanisms to maintain system operation during component failures
Incorporate real-time monitoring and predictive maintenance to minimize downtime
Design modular components to allow quick replacement and repair
Establish operational procedures and safety plans to reduce human error and operational interruptions
Use robust and fault-tolerant system architecture to handle unexpected faults gracefully
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement redundancy and failover mechanisms to maintain system operation during component failures

potential_sources: Redundancy mechanisms may require coordination with monitoring systems
Failover logic must interface with operational procedures
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on logical independence by isolating failure handling within redundancy and failover mechanisms, minimizing coupling with other DPs. Coordination points exist but are manageable through clear interface definitions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes that redundancy can be implemented without excessive cost or complexity
Risk that failover mechanisms may introduce new failure modes if not properly designed
Assumes operational policies support and do not conflict with automated failover processes
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including real-time monitoring as a supporting DP to enhance failover effectiveness and early fault detection.

im_boundary.name: Safety Services
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for ensuring system safety, including redundancy and failover to maintain operation during failures

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Sends alerts when failover or redundancy mechanisms activate to maintain safety.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives system status data and sends health reports related to redundancy and failover.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communicates with other modules to coordinate failover and redundancy actions.

module_containment_scores.notes: Very broad module; contains DP but diffusion is high due to encompassing many unrelated functions.
Contains physical components and some failover, but redundancy mechanisms may span beyond vehicle hardware.
Focuses on remote control; redundancy/failover is peripheral and would diffuse heavily outside.
Highly cohesive containment of redundancy and failover mechanisms with minimal diffusion outside.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Safety Services is the most specific and cohesive module to contain redundancy and failover mechanisms, minimizing diffusion and aligning with safety responsibilities.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Vehicle module if hardware-level failover is tightly integrated.
Coordination complexity with Teleoperation and NGHE Solution modules may require well-defined interfaces.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP of implementing redundancy and failover mechanisms directly addresses the FR of achieving operational availability equal to or greater than the customer-defined target. The DP logically supports maintaining system operation during component failures, which is essential to meet the availability criterion.

diffusion_risks: Potential overlap with Vehicle module if hardware-level failover is tightly integrated.
Coordination complexity with Teleoperation and NGHE Solution modules may require well-defined interfaces.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The Safety Services module has a high containment score (9) and low diffusion score (2), indicating it is a highly cohesive and appropriate boundary for the DP. This minimizes diffusion risk and aligns well with safety responsibilities. However, some diffusion risk remains due to necessary coordination with other modules.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the FR through the IM is feasible by monitoring the Safety Services module's operation, alerts, and telemetry data to confirm redundancy and failover mechanisms maintain availability ≥ 0.97 over the evaluation period.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the Safety Services IM is clear and well-justified, with explicit interfaces supporting coordination and alerting functions necessary for maintaining operational availability.

recommended_revisions: Define clear interface contracts and coordination protocols between Safety Services and Vehicle, Teleoperation, and NGHE Solution modules to mitigate diffusion risks.
Document hardware-level failover responsibilities to clarify overlap between Safety Services and Vehicle modules.
Include verification procedures that leverage Safety Services telemetry and alerts to demonstrate compliance with availability acceptance criteria.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with strong justification and clear allocation of responsibilities. The Safety Services module is a highly cohesive implementation boundary with manageable diffusion risks that can be mitigated through defined interfaces and protocols. Verification feasibility is established, and recommended revisions focus on interface clarity and documentation rather than further decomposition. Given the high safety criticality, maintaining a clear and stable architectural boundary is preferable to further decomposition at this stage.

revise.why: To address diffusion risks and coordination complexity, revise the Safety Services module's interface contracts and coordination protocols with Vehicle, Teleoperation, and NGHE Solution modules, and document hardware-level failover responsibilities clearly.

assumptions: The DP of redundancy and failover mechanisms fully satisfies the FR of operational availability.
The Safety Services module can effectively encapsulate the DP with minimal diffusion risk.
Verification through telemetry and alerts is sufficient to demonstrate compliance with availability criteria.
open_risks: Potential overlap with Vehicle module at hardware failover level.
Coordination complexity with Teleoperation and NGHE Solution modules may introduce integration challenges.
evidence_needed: Interface contracts and coordination protocols documentation.
Verification procedures leveraging Safety Services telemetry and alerts.
